zh.Kinderton cum Hulme, Cheshire, England: 英國爾金德頓 姆 赫爾姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Hatton於1075年出生在英國爾哈頓時，他的父親Wolfric de Hatton38歲，母親Emma de Daresbury37歲。他於1100年與Emma Darcsbury在英國爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Kinderton cum Hulme, Cheshire, England: 英国爾金德頓 姆 赫爾姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Hatton于1075年出生在英国爾哈頓时，他的父亲Wolfric de Hatton38岁，母亲Emma de Daresbury37岁。他于1100年与Emma Darcsbury在英国爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hatton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Hatten
meanings: English: variant of   Hatton  . This surname is also common among African Americans.
North German: habitational name from the name of an area of marshland between Oldenburg and Bremen.

variants.name: Hattan
meanings: English and Scottish (Argyll): variant of   Hatton  .

variants.name: Aton
meanings: variant of Hatton , with loss of initial H-.
topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)).

variants.name: Heath
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Hatton was born in 1075, in Hatton, Cheshire, England, his father, Wolfric de Hatton, was 38 and his mother, Emma de Daresbury, was 37. He married Emma Darcsbury in 1100, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Hatton est né en 1075, à Hatton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Wolfric de Hatton, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Emma de Daresbury, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Emma Darcsbury en 1100, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Hatton 1075 in Hatton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wolfric de Hatton 38 und seine Mutter Emma de Daresbury 37. Er hat 1100 in Cheshire, England Emma Darcsbury geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Hatton è nato il nel 1075 nel Hatton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Wolfric de Hatton, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Emma de Daresbury, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Emma Darcsbury nel 1100, nel Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Kinderton cum Hulme, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアキンデルトン クウム ハルメ
ja.lifeSummary: William de Hattonが1075年にイギリスチェシュアイアハットオンで生まれた時，父Wolfric de Hattonは38歳，母Emma de Daresburyは37歳であった。1100年にイギリスチェシュアイアでEmma Darcsburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Hatton(이)가 1075년 영국 체실르 해턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wolfric de Hatton(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma de Daresbury(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1100년 영국 체실르에서 Emma Darcsbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Hatton nasceu em 1075, em Hatton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Wolfric de Hatton, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Emma de Daresbury, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Emma Darcsbury em 1100, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Hatton родился в 1075 г. в местности хаттон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Wolfric de Hatton было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma de Daresbury, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma Darcsbury в 1100 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер в его родном городе.

sm.Bet 1100 and 1138: mai le 1100 e o'o I le 1138

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Hatton nació en 1075, en Hatton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Wolfric de Hatton, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Emma de Daresbury, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Emma Darcsbury en 1100, en Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió en su pueblo.
